paper_title: Radiocarbon Dating of Polychrome Layers in Portuguese Sculptures

methods.description: This method involves the identification and analysis of the first polychromy layers on sculptures to understand their painting techniques and materials.

steps.description: Thorough macroscopic and microscopic observation and documentation of the sculptures' surface.
steps.searchable_summary: document sculpture surfaces using macroscopic and microscopic techniques

steps.description: Selection, documentation, and removal of paint samples from different polychrome areas of each sculpture.
steps.searchable_summary: select and document paint samples from sculptures

steps.description: Examination of paint samples as cross-sections by optical microscopy using visible light and ultraviolet radiation.
steps.searchable_summary: examine paint cross-sections using optical microscopy

steps.description: Identification of painting materials using micro-Raman spectroscopy, micro-EDXRF, and SEM-EDX.
steps.searchable_summary: identify painting materials using spectroscopy techniques

steps.description: Study of binders using FTIR-ATR to confirm the natural origin of the binder.

methods.description: This method describes the preparation of samples for radiocarbon dating, focusing on isolating the carbon signature of lead white and binders.

steps.description: Samples were prepared following a two-step isolation procedure to gain both the lead white and binder 14C signature.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for radiocarbon dating using isolation procedure

steps.description: Lead white thermally decomposes to lead oxide at 350 °C, forming CO2 which can be captured and measured.
steps.searchable_summary: thermally decompose lead white to capture CO2

steps.description: Other carbonates removed by acid hydrolysis to date the carbon fraction of the binder.
Solvent extraction step to remove free reactive species from the natural binder.
steps.searchable_summary: perform solvent extraction to clean binder

steps.description: Larger samples split into two fractions for cleaning by acetone and ethanol, and evaluation of the two approaches.
steps.searchable_summary: clean samples using acetone and ethanol
